summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting that included an executive session to discuss employee appointment/compensation, addressed multiple property nuisance resolutions with some confusion over resolution numbering, heard community announcements about tornado recovery efforts, and discussed upcoming community events. The board also accepted a firefighter resignation, appointed a volunteer firefighter, and heard a presentation from a resident interested in starting a winery/event venue on Smith Road.
decisions: Motion to amend agenda moving item M to A1 - passed
Motion to adopt agenda as amended - passed
Motion to enter executive session at 7:03 PM for employee appointment/compensation discussion - passed
Motion to approve payment of bills (Oct 11-24, 2022) - passed (Hodges abstained)
Motion to accept resignation of firefighter paramedic Wes Hartoyne effective 10/19/2022 - passed
Motion to appoint Hayden Johnson as volunteer firefighter effective 10/25/2022 - passed
Resolution R055-2022 declaring 6972 Goshen Road a nuisance - passed
Resolution R056-2022 (originally voted as Willow Ridge Court) rescinded due to resolution/address mismatch
Resolution R056-2022 declaring 1338 O'Canyonville Road a nuisance - passed
Resolution R057-2022 declaring 7000 Willow Ridge Court a nuisance - passed
Resolution R058-2022 declaring 1499 Fay Road a nuisance - passed
action_items: Lisa/Trustees to talk to Harry Holbert about Matthew Hammond's winery/event venue proposal on Smith Road and schedule a follow-up meeting
Bob (new administrator), Harry Holbert, and trustees to coordinate meeting with Matthew Hammond regarding zoning for proposed winery/vineyard/event venue
Chad to coordinate with schools (Spalding, Marquardt, others) on snowplow painting project for parade
Ed to attend meeting regarding tower generator update (scheduled for next day at 1:00 PM)
Ed to follow up on temporary fire station door delivery expected week of November 14th
Insurance company to review Station 18 architect/construction manager contracts
CPAAA to place coat collection bin in township lobby for Shop with a Cop
Hannah from Clermont County to arrange BSAC removal of file of faults material starting November 7th
Stephen/Board to continue pressuring state legislature for tornado relief/EMA funding reimbursement
key_amounts: $170,000+ distributed by long-term recovery committee to approximately 70 tornado-affected families
notable_topics: Tornado recovery updates - long-term recovery committee helping 70 families, rebuilding ongoing nearly 4 months later
State tornado relief funding advocacy - calls to Governor, State Rep Adam Byrd meeting with Governor
Multiple property nuisance resolutions (6972 Goshen Rd, 1338 O'Canyonville Rd, 7000 Willow Ridge Ct, 1499 Fay Rd)
Resident Matthew Hammond proposing winery/vineyard/floral farm/wedding venue on Smith Road - zoning discussion
CPAAA Shop with a Cop coat collection
Service department snowplow painting project with local schools
Firefighter resignation (Wes Hartoyne) and volunteer firefighter appointment (Hayden Johnson)
Fire station temporary quarters update and Station 18 rebuild progress
Polly Fair/Lions Club event November 19th and Light of Goshen Parade at 4:30 PM
Steve's last meeting before moving to Hamilton Township
Wood Street tornado repair construction activity
attendees: Matthew Hammond (public speaker, prospective business owner)
Harry Holbert (Zoning, absent due to illness)
notes: Significant confusion occurred during the nuisance resolution votes when agenda items did not match the resolution numbers and addresses on the actual resolution documents, requiring a rescission and re-vote. Steve (fiscal officer) was recognized at his final meeting before departing for Hamilton Township. The Spaulding family was mentioned in the opening prayer. A new township administrator named Bob was introduced as starting at the end of the week. The transcript appears to cut off or become garbled near the end during the final nuisance resolutions.
title: BOT 10-25-22.mp4
